question_text: Which statement correctly describes what happens when an object with a density of 0.8 g/cm³ is placed in liquid water with a density of 1.0 g/cm³?

options.label: A
options.text: The object will sink to the bottom of the container

options.label: B
options.text: The object will float partially submerged in the water

options.label: C
options.text: The object will remain suspended in the middle of the water

options.label: D
options.text: The object will float at the surface without touching the water

correct_answer: The object will float partially submerged in the water
explanation: When an object's density is less than the density of a liquid, it floats. Since 0.8 g/cm³ is less than 1.0 g/cm³, the object floats. It floats partially submerged because only enough of the object needs to be underwater to displace a mass of water equal to the object's own mass.
